eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance maintains a narrow edge in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential winner, reflecting his role as heir apparent in the Trump administration and recent Iowa caucus outreach receiving strong Republican backing. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has surged closer following his high-profile Vatican summit with Pope Leo XIV and a viral White House address outlining an optimistic vision for America, generating buzz among conservatives and highlighting his diplomatic stature. California Governor Gavin Newsom holds steady as the leading Democratic contender amid an open primary field post-Harris. This tight contest among top probabilities near 20% underscores early-stage uncertainty, with 2026 midterm results in battleground states, administration achievements, and potential Trump endorsements poised to create separation before 2028 primaries.

markets.question: Will Anthropic be acquired before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if credible reporting confirms that any entity enters into an agreement to acquire the listed company by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket places Cursor at a 74% implied probability of acquisition before 2027, propelled by SpaceX's April 21 announcement of a $60 billion option to buy the AI-powered code editor, positioning it as a key asset in the competitive developer tools landscape against GitHub Copilot and emerging large language model integrations. Caesars Entertainment follows closely at 71%, amid ongoing takeover talks with billionaire Tilman Fertitta valued around $7 billion since March, reflecting casino sector consolidation. Viking Therapeutics trades at 56% on biotech M&A speculation in GLP-1 obesity drugs, with high volume signaling big pharma interest. Watch Q2 earnings from GitLab and Zoom, plus potential regulatory scrutiny on the SpaceX-Cursor deal, as catalysts through year-end resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Melanie Stansbury commands trader consensus at 90.5% implied probability to win New Mexico's 1st Congressional District House seat, driven by the urban Albuquerque-based district's strong Democratic lean (historical D+8 margins) and her proven incumbency advantage after wide victories in prior cycles. Recent FEC filings highlight Stansbury's dominant fundraising edge over an uncompetitive Republican primary field ahead of the June 2 primaries, with no high-profile GOP challengers emerging to date. This safe Democratic stronghold shows no notable shifts in the past 30 days, underscoring limited paths to an upset. Realistic challenges include a scandal hitting Stansbury, a surprise GOP recruit gaining national backing, or broader midterm turnout surges favoring Republicans, though structural barriers persist through the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve has maintained its federal funds target range at 3.50%–3.75% through the April 28–29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting persistent inflation pressures evidenced by March CPI rising 3.3% year-over-year and a resilient labor market with April nonfarm payrolls adding 115,000 jobs while unemployment held steady at 4.3%. The March dot plot projected a median rate of 3.4% by end-2026, signaling gradual easing contingent on disinflation progress, amid 10-year Treasury yields near 4.40%. Traders weigh these dynamics against upcoming catalysts, including April CPI data on May 12 and the June 16–17 FOMC, where softening labor indicators or renewed price pressures could shift market-implied rate paths.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Mexico City be 20°C on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Benito Juárez International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Benito Juárez International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/mx/mexico-city/MMMX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Ben Griffin win the 2026 Truist Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Hall, Lucas Glover, and Patrick Cantlay share near-identical 48-49% implied probabilities as trader consensus favorites for the Truist Championship winner, reflecting their strong mid-pack positioning through early Round 3 action at Quail Hollow Club amid a tightly bunched leaderboard. Persistent thunderstorms over the past 48 hours suspended Round 1 Thursday evening and delayed Friday's resumption until 10:30 a.m., yielding uneven conditions that limited scoring separation—Sungjae Im leads at -9 after 36 holes, but Hall (66-72, -4 thru early R3), Glover (68-72, gaining strokes thru 7), and Cantlay (72-68 rebound, surging thru 7) lurk within five shots with 30-plus holes remaining in the no-cut Signature Event. Quail Hollow's long par-71 layout favors precise iron play and putting, where Glover's veteran accuracy and Cantlay's course familiarity amplify upset potential in volatile weather.

markets.question: Will G2 Esports qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for MSI 2026 qualifiers reflects ongoing regional Split 2 regular seasons and Road to MSI tournaments, with no teams yet locked into the 11 slots—two each from LCK, LPL, LEC, LCS, and LCP, plus one from CBLOL. In LCK, Hanwha Life Esports leads at 10-1, bolstered by strong macro play and recent sweeps, ahead of Gen.G at 9-3 and T1; LCS sees undefeated Cloud9 and Team Liquid dominating with consistent early-game leads. LEC frontrunners Team Vitality (8-1) and Karmine Corp hold advantages via superior bot lanes, while LPL's LGD Gaming and LNG Esports top early Split 2 standings. LCP's Team Secret Whales sit perfect at 6-0. Playoffs and Road to MSI brackets in late May will decide seeds, with patch meta shifts and roster health key variables.

The token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable. Announcements alone do not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Hylo (https://x.com/hylo_so), however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will there be exactly 3 earthquakes of magnitude 6.5 or higher worldwide from May 11 - 17?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 6.5 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between May 11, 2026, 12:00 AM ET, and May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

This market may not resolve until the timeframe of this market has concluded. If a qualifying earthquake has been recorded on the final day, this market may remain open for 24 hours to allow for revisions to the recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

markets.question: Will Benoni Mendes win the 2026 Minas Gerais gubernatorial election?
markets.description:
The Minas Gerais gubernatorial election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, with a runoff on October 25, 2026, if no candidate receives a majority of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder governors appointed by any means before the specified election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

Quaest's April 22-26 survey showed him topping first-round scenarios at 30-37%, with double-digit second-round advantages over challengers like Senate President Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB) and former Belo Horizonte Mayor Alexandre Kalil (PDT). The latest Doxa poll reinforces his edge at 28-30%, though Kalil rose to 21-24% while Pacheco slipped to 6%, and Vice-Governor Mateus Simões (PSD), Zema's continuity choice, polled at 7%. Pacheco's 17.5% market share reflects his institutional profile amid alliance talks, with Gabriel Azevedo (MDB) at 13.4% gaining pre-candidate buzz.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Nvidia Data Center Revenue above 50B in Q1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Nvidia’s data center revenue for the first fiscal quarter of 2027, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.
The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released, and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 
 
If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market. 

The resolution source for this market is Nvidia’s official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

markets.question: RB Leipzig vs. FC St. Pauli 1910: O/U 8.5 Total Corners
markets.description: In the upcoming Bundesliga game between RB Leipzig and FC St. Pauli 1910, scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 9:30 AM ET: This market will resolve to "Over" if there are 9 or more total corners awarded in this game (combining corners for both teams). If the total number of corners is fewer than 9, this market will resolve to "Under". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. This market refers only to corners awarded within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Corners awarded during extra time or penalty shootouts do not count. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: RB Leipzig, entrenched in third place in the Bundesliga standings, host relegation-battling FC St. Pauli—17th with a dismal recent form of L-D-L-D-L-L—in a pivotal season finale at Red Bull Arena. Leipzig's high-pressing style generates 5.5 corners per game overall and 6.1 at home, while St. Pauli concede 5.0 on average, contributing to matches averaging 9.6 total corners. Their January head-to-head drew 1-1 with only 7 corners, but broader trends suggest higher volume amid St. Pauli's injury crisis sidelining James Sands, Mathias Pereira Lage, and others, likely forcing a deep defensive block that amplifies Leipzig's set-piece pressure and trader focus on over outcomes.

markets.question: Will Zelenskyy post 140-159 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Volodymyr Zelenskyy (@ZelenskyyUa), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's "What will be said on ICEMAN?" market heavily favors "Daddy" at 85% implied probability and "Kawhi/Leonard" at 62%, reflecting Drake's signature blend of fatherhood themes, Toronto sports nods, and personal storytelling on his ninth studio album, set for streaming debut May 15 via OVO Sound and Republic Records. Amid no confirmed tracklist leaks—just unverified zine hints from late April—these frontrunners stem from Drake's recent cryptic rollout, including Toronto ice block stunts and a massive 60.1 million Spotify streams yesterday without new singles, signaling sustained fan momentum. Lower odds on beef callbacks like "Kendrick/Lamar" (22%) suggest traders anticipate closure over escalation, though post-release transcripts from Spotify will dictate resolutions by June 30 if delayed; watch for last-minute previews that could shift trajectories before chart battles.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump announce Todd Blanche as the next United States Attorney General by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual Donald Trump announces as his pick to be United States Attorney General. 

An announcement from Donald Trump or the Trump administration stating their intent to nominate a specific individual for United States Attorney General will suffice to resolve this market, regardless of whether a formal nomination actually occurs.

Qualifying announcements must explicitly present the relevant individual as the nominee or future nominee for Attorney General. Announcements of acting or interim appointments, or announcements which merely reveal potential candidates, will not qualify. Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be United States Attorney General will also suffice to resolve this market. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

If Donald Trump does not announce his pick for the next United States Attorney General by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No announcement by June 30".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump and the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Lower odds for Ken Paxton stem from his active U.S. Senate campaign in Texas, and Todd Blanche's acting role carries confirmation hurdles amid ongoing DOJ probes into Trump grievances, underscoring uncertainty in the nomination timeline.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Mexico City be 22°C on May 10?

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Benito Juárez International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/mx/mexico-city/MMMX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

eventMetadata.context_description: The latest extended forecast from Mexico's Servicio Meteorológico Nacional (SMN) projects maximum temperatures of 30–35°C for Mexico City on May 10, amid a persisting heat wave or "onda de calor" that has elevated highs to 29–31°C as of May 8, solidifying trader consensus at 93.5% implied probability for 25°C or higher. This positioning reflects seasonal dry conditions in May—when average highs peak near 27°C on the 10th—combined with a high-pressure ridge suppressing clouds and boosting solar heating despite the city's 2,240-meter elevation. Model consensus from SMN aligns with minimal cooling influences, though isolated afternoon chubascos (5–25 mm rain) introduce slight uncertainty. Realistic challenges include an unexpected surge in cloud cover or a premature frontal boundary, potentially capping peaks below 25°C, but current steering patterns make this improbable; watch SMN's daily updates for refinements.

events.title: UFC: Who will Charles Oliveira fight next?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter that Charles Oliveira is officially announced to face in a UFC bout.

Resolution for this market will be based on the next UFC fighter that Charles Oliveira is officially announced to fight, regardless of whether the fight ends up taking place.

Only official announcements from the UFC, which include a scheduled date for the bout, will count. Announcements with no date or which do not confirm that the fight is official, speculation, or other unofficial announcements will not count.

If Charles Oliveira is officially announced to fight any non-listed fighter or no qualifying announcement is made by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Charles Oliveira's dominant BMF title win over Max Holloway at UFC 326 two months ago vaulted him to No. 3 in lightweight rankings, but with no official next opponent announced amid his new eight-fight UFC contract, traders split consensus tightly between Dan Hooker (40%) and Arman Tsarukyan (34%). Hooker's ongoing callouts and heated post-fight exchanges—firing back at Oliveira's emotional victory and past rumored matchups—fuel striker-vs-submission intrigue, while Tsarukyan's prior split-decision edge over Oliveira and recent push for a BMF rematch position him as a top contender threat. This closely contested market reflects lightweight volatility, recent form, and stylistic risks in potential title eliminators.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 93%, reflecting the absence of any DOJ indictment or charges against Les Wexner despite ongoing scrutiny over his past ties to Jeffrey Epstein. Wexner, the L Brands founder who granted Epstein power of attorney and a Manhattan townhouse, testified in a February 2026 House Oversight Committee deposition that he was "conned" by Epstein, denied knowledge of crimes, and severed ties after discovering theft—claims unchallenged by federal prosecutors to date. A civil lawsuit filed by Epstein victims in early April alleges Wexner enabled abuse, but he has never faced criminal charges, with unredacted Epstein files merely labeling him a co-conspirator without triggering DOJ action. No recent federal probes or announcements signal movement by June 30, amid potential statute of limitations hurdles.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Farmer" during events with Xi Jinping?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to meet with Xi Jinping from May 14 to May 15, 2026 (https://www.cnn.com/2026/05/04/china/china-us-talks-iran-intl-hnk). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during his appearance at events featuring Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

This market is explicitly about events featuring both Donald Trump and Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. All such events featuring both on these dates count. Speeches, events, or comments which feature one named individual but not the other, will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the events contain a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this visit is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are set for a bilateral summit in Beijing on May 14-15, their first in-person meeting since October 2025, amid US-China frictions over tariffs, rare earths, Taiwan, technology transfers, and the Iran conflict. In the past 48 hours, Trump praised Xi as a "friend," highlighted cordial Iran discussions, and noted significant ongoing trade, signaling a potentially positive tone despite policy tensions. China reiterated stable bilateral ties while prioritizing Taiwan ahead of the talks, with US advance teams already on the ground. Key uncertainties include progress on trade barriers and Iran de-escalation signals, which could influence Trump's phrasing during joint statements or press interactions resolving the market.

markets.question: Will "Michael" be the April film with the highest domestic gross on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the title of the film released in April 2026 with the highest domestic gross between opening and May 30, 2026 according to "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page.

In the event of an exact tie the film that comes first alphabetically will be considered the winner.

If there is no final data available by June 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

events.title: Highest Domestically Grossing April Film on May 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the title of the film released in April 2026 with the highest domestic gross between opening and May 30, 2026 according to "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page.

In the event of an exact tie the film that comes first alphabetically will be considered the winner.

If there is no final data available by June 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie commands 99% market-implied odds as the highest domestically grossing April 2026 release, propelled by its record-shattering $131 million opening weekend and sustained legs that pushed domestic grosses past $405 million by early May—more than double runner-up Michael's $204 million haul from its late-month biopic debut. Illumination's family-friendly animation, riding Nintendo's franchise momentum from the 2023 hit, delivered strong word-of-mouth, repeat viewings, and premium format dominance despite middling 42% Rotten Tomatoes scores. Challengers like You, Me & Tuscany ($19 million) and niche titles Mother Mary or Wasteland Cop trail far behind with limited theatrical runs. Realistic upsets would require unprecedented multipliers for laggards or a Mario collapse, improbable given current trajectories; traders eye May 31 grosses amid ongoing holdovers.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $296 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Amazon's (AMZN) share price hovers near $273 as of May 9, 2026, propelled by a 27% April rally after Q1 earnings on April 29 beat estimates with $181.5 billion in revenue (+17% year-over-year), record 13.1% operating margin, and AWS revenue surging 28% to $37.6 billion on AI-driven cloud demand. Net income hit $30.3 billion, boosted by $16.8 billion in Anthropic investment gains, though a $200 billion FY2026 capex plan for data centers capped immediate upside. Trader sentiment prices in sustained AWS acceleration and e-commerce margin expansion amid logistics network scaling, with analyst consensus targets at $313. Watch Q2 earnings on July 30 for revenue trends and capex updates influencing end-May positioning.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Seoul be 10°C on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Korea Meteorological Administration's latest short-term forecast, updated May 8, projects Seoul's May 10 minimum temperature at 10°C amid clear skies and light winds favoring radiative cooling overnight, driving trader sentiment toward lower outcomes like 10°C (27%) and 11°C (36.5%). However, model discrepancies—ECMWF ensembles suggesting potential dips to 8°C around 03:00, BBC at 13°C—create uncertainty reflected in tight odds for 12°C (24.5%) and 13°C (20.5%), as small variations in cloud timing or urban heat retention could differentiate them. Recent cool nights, including 9°C on May 5, align with climatological May lows near 12°C; watch KMA's May 9 update for refined guidance ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects the narrow spread in latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts, projecting Istanbul's highest temperature on May 10 at 21-23°C amid a warming trend following a cool early-May northerly airflow that capped prior highs below 15°C. The Turkish State Meteorological Service (MGM) guidance supports partly cloudy skies with highs clustering around 22°C, tempered by Marmara Sea breezes limiting peak heating. Differentiating factors include model disagreements on afternoon cloud cover and urban heat island amplification across Istanbul stations, yielding genuine uncertainty in the exact integer degree. Watch MGM's May 9 update and hourly data for refined peak timing, as resolution hinges on official airport observations.

markets.question: Will no company have an AI model hit 1550 on Chatbot Arena in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed entity, which is the first to reach an Arena Score of 1550+ on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Results from the "Text Arena" section on the leaderboard/text tab of https://lmarena.ai/ with the style control unchecked (https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/overall-no-style-control) will be used to resolve this market.

If no company's model reaches 1550+ Arena Score by the specified time, this market will resolve to "None in 2026".

If the first model to reach a 1550+ Arena Score belongs to any company other than the listed ones, this market will be resolved to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source becomes unavailable, the market will remain open until it is accessible again. If it becomes permanently unavailable, resolution will be based on another credible source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no AI model reaching 1550 Elo on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena in 2026 at 59% implied probability, reflecting the leaderboard's stagnation around 1504 Elo for Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7, released April 16 after topping code and text arenas. Recent launches like OpenAI's GPT-5.5 (late April) and xAI's Grok 4.3 delivered benchmark gains in reasoning and specialized tasks such as legal analysis but trailed in blind human-voted Arena Elo due to evaluation lag, keeping tops under 1510. Google's Gemini 3.5 Flash hit 1470+ anonymously last week, bolstering its 18% odds amid vast compute resources. With seven months left, traders anticipate scaling plateaus, regulatory hurdles on massive training runs, and no confirmed frontier releases to bridge the 46-point gap before year-end.

Recent software releases, including GPT-5.5 Instant on May 5 for enhanced personalization and new voice intelligence models in the API on May 7, bolster capabilities for embedded AI but do not qualify as the anticipated consumer product. Competitive dynamics with Meta's wearables and Google's Gemini hardware intensify focus on form factors like earbuds or AI phones, with mass production leaks pointing to 2027 availability; watch for developer conferences or direct reveals before year-end resolution.

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after June 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The summary for the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee meeting for June 2026 is scheduled to be released on June 18, 2026.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the Bank Rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of England's June 2026 meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of England (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the Bank Rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of England's statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the start date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign an 84.5% implied probability to no change in the Bank of England Bank Rate at the June 18, 2026, Monetary Policy Committee meeting, reflecting the MPC's 8-1 decision to hold steady at 3.75% on April 30 despite March 2026 CPI inflation rising to 3.3% from 3.0%, driven by higher energy and motor fuel costs. This positioning underscores trader consensus on persistent price pressures above the 2% target—exacerbated by pass-through effects from global oil volatility—yet tempered by softening labor market signals, including February unemployment at 4.9%. The 16% odds on a 25 basis point increase capture the hawkish dissenter's view and BoE warnings of elevated inflation risks ahead, with April CPI data due mid-May as the key near-term catalyst.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the TX-38 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: The TX-38 House seat, an open race after incumbent Wesley Hunt's U.S. Senate bid, favors Republicans at 81.5% trader consensus due to the district's entrenched R+12 Cook PVI and consistent 25-point GOP margins in 2022 and 2024 general elections. Democratic nominee Melissa McDonough, who lost 37%-63% to Hunt last cycle, faces a steep uphill battle in the Houston-area suburbs. On the GOP side, Trump-endorsed mortgage banker Jon Bonck, the top primary vote-getter at 47.7% on March 3, heads to the May 26 runoff against Shelly deZevallos; forecasters like Cook Political rate it Solid Republican. No major developments in the past 30 days have shifted the dynamics ahead of the November 3 general election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Instituto Nacional de Meteorologia (INMET) forecasts, Brazil's authoritative weather agency, project a maximum temperature of 28°C in São Paulo today under mostly cloudy conditions with light northerly winds, driving trader consensus to near-certainty on this outcome as short-range model ensembles converge tightly. Persistent cloud cover limits solar insolation, capping daytime heating despite May's typical autumnal highs around 23–24°C; current upper-air patterns favor stability without convective outbursts. Scenarios challenging this include unexpected afternoon clearing allowing greater radiative warming or a wind shift enhancing warm advection, though ensemble probabilities remain below 1% per INMET guidance. Monitor hourly observations from official stations like Congonhas Airport for intraday shifts ahead of evening resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alphabet's commanding 86% implied probability as the second-largest company by market capitalization at May 31 stems from its current $4.81 trillion valuation—solidly ahead of Apple's $4.31 trillion—bolstered by late April Q1 earnings that showcased 63% Google Cloud revenue growth to $20 billion and a doubled backlog to $460 billion, propelling shares up over 7% in a day and cementing its overtake of Apple. NVIDIA's top spot at $5.23 trillion faces 10% odds of slipping to second amid pre-earnings volatility before its May 20 fiscal Q1 release, while Apple's recent 2% daily gain narrows the gap modestly but insufficiently per trader consensus. Lower probabilities for Microsoft, Saudi Aramco, Tesla, Amazon, and Broadcom reflect their distant trailing market caps below $3.1 trillion, underscoring Big Tech dominance in capex-fueled AI expansion.

markets.question: Will Lars Boje Mathiesen be the next prime minister of Denmark after the 2026 parliamentary elections?
markets.description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of Denmark following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Denmark's inconclusive snap general election on March 24, 2026, where Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats secured the largest bloc in the fragmented Folketing despite their weakest result in over a century, coalition negotiations have dragged into their sixth week amid policy disputes over welfare, immigration, and green initiatives. Trader consensus favors Frederiksen at 64.5% implied probability, reflecting her party's persistent poll lead around 21% and history of centrist "rainbow" coalitions enabling minority governments. On May 8, King Frederik X tasked Venstre leader Troels Lund Poulsen—current deputy prime minister—with exploratory talks for a center-right coalition excluding Social Democrats, boosting his odds to 15.7%, while Moderates' Lars Løkke Rasmussen at 12.1% remains a pivotal kingmaker. Prolonged uncertainty risks snap polls by summer.

The 24-year-old switch-stancer from Kings MMA made weight at 185 lbs Thursday without issues, as did the 35-year-old Diaz at 186 lbs post his recent UFC decision win over Djorden Santos. markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

EUR/USD trades around 1.1780, supported by euro strength but pressured by US safe-haven dollar demand amid US-Iran tensions and elevated oil prices hurting Eurozone importers. Sticky inflation on both sides tempers rate cut bets; traders eye US April CPI release May 12, June FOMC/ECB meetings, and Q2 GDP data as pivotal for exchange rate trajectory through 2026.

markets.question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $566,000 and $572,000 on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on May 31, 2026. If no data for May 31 is released by June 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/36)

markets.question: Will Erling Haaland be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season?
markets.description:

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025-26 EPL season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No". 

Goals scored in Premier League matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., FA Cup, Carabao Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market. 

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Austin be between 62-63°F on May 10?

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Austin-Bergstrom International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Austin-Bergstrom International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/austin/KAUS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors the Partido Liberal (PL) at 82% to win the most seats among 54 up for grabs in Brazil's October 4 Senate election, reflecting opposition momentum amid President Lula's challenges. The Senate's April 29 rejection of Lula's Supreme Court nominee—the first in 132 years—highlights right-wing strength, with PL targeting 25-30 seats through aggressive candidate recruitment backed by the Bolsonaro family. State-level polls show PL leading in key races like São Paulo and Rio de Janeiro, bolstered by Flávio Bolsonaro's tight presidential polls and PL's gains in recent congressional party shifts. Low historical Senate turnout favors the mobilized conservative base, positioning PL ahead of allies like Republicanos (23%) while left-leaning PT lags at 0.3%.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid a fragile ceasefire in the Strait of Hormuz following recent US-Iran exchanges of fire, diplomats continue indirect negotiations via proposal exchanges after the first in-person talks in Islamabad last month, where US Vice President JD Vance led alongside special envoy Steve Witkoff and advisor Jared Kushner, meeting Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi. Iran is reviewing a US peace plan as President Trump expresses optimism for a non-nuclear deal, but no second round date or attendees have been confirmed. Iran's one-month negotiation deadline could prompt escalation to higher officials like the US Secretary of State or Iranian Supreme Leader envoys, shaping trader consensus on participation amid de-escalation signals and mediation by Pakistan.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner leads trader consensus at 51% implied probability for the 2026 Men’s Wimbledon winner, bolstered by his World No. 1 ranking and dominant clay-court form, including a straight-sets Madrid Open title over Alexander Zverev last week and a comfortable second-round win over Sebastian Ofner in ongoing Rome. markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lagos be 34°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ng/lagos/DNMM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 100% implied probability to a highest temperature of 34°C in Lagos on May 9, 2026, driven by official hourly observations from Murtala Muhammed International Airport (MMIA)—the market's resolution source via Weather Underground—which peaked at 34°C around mid-afternoon amid partly cloudy skies. NiMet's latest bulletin forecasted isolated thunderstorms with light to moderate rains across southern Nigeria, including Lagos, capping convective heating after morning stability and introducing cooling via cloud cover and precipitation. This aligns with May climatology, where highs average 31–32°C but recent dry spells buoyed peaks to 32–34°C over May 7–9. Realistic challenges include an improbable late-evening heat burst before final showers, though current METAR data shows temperatures dropping below 30°C with rising humidity. Final Wunderground daily archive expected soon.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls, including AtlasIntel/Bloomberg and Datafolha from late April, show Senator Flávio Bolsonaro holding a slim first-round lead over incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva at around 44-46% to 41-45%, with statistical ties in simulated runoffs, mirroring Polymarket's trader consensus where Flávio edges Lula 43% to 39.5%. This tightness stems from Brazil's polarized electorate: Flávio inherits his father Jair Bolsonaro's loyal conservative base despite the ex-president's imprisonment, while Lula's incumbency benefits from left-wing turnout but faces headwinds from rising disapproval ratings above 50% amid economic pressures. Jair Bolsonaro's jail term reduction last week has further boosted right-wing momentum. Separation could arise from upcoming party conventions, regional polling shifts in swing states like São Paulo and Minas Gerais, or debate performances ahead of the October 4 first round and potential runoff.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-21 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-21 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Toronto be 15°C on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.title: Grenoble Foot 38 vs. ES Troyes AC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 2 game, scheduled for May 9 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Troyes lead the Ligue 2 table after 32 matches with 19 wins, 64 points, and a +24 goal difference, driving trader focus on their automatic promotion push with six games remaining, while mid-table Grenoble languish in 13th on 33 points from six wins and 15 draws, comfortably safe from relegation. Recent form underscores the gap: Troyes posted a vital 1-0 clean-sheet home win over Boulogne on April 18 but dropped points in a 2-1 loss at Rodez and 2-2 draw at Montpellier earlier in the month; Grenoble suffered a narrow 2-1 defeat at Montpellier on April 17 amid their draw-heavy campaign. Troyes face absences with goalkeeper Nicolas Lemaître (cruciate ligament tear) and center-back Paolo Gozzi (broken fibula until late June), yet their attacking output (56 goals) and away resilience favor them at Stade des Alpes, where Grenoble holds a modest home record. Head-to-head favors Troyes slightly in recent meetings.

markets.question: Will Meta have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between LOS and Fluxo W7M in the CBLOL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 9 at 12:00PM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to the remake. If the first blood did not occur prior to the remake, resolution will be based on the remade game.

If Game 1 is completed but first blood never occurred, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: LOS vs Fluxo W7M (BO5) - CBLOL Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between LOS and Fluxo W7M in the CBLOL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "LOS" if LOS win the match against Fluxo W7M.

This market will resolve to "Fluxo W7M" if Fluxo W7M win the match against LOS.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: LOS enters the CBLOL Split 1 2026 playoffs upper bracket quarterfinals BO5 against Fluxo W7M as slight favorites after a 2-1 regular season win over them on May 3, boosted by new jungler Curse's strong debut alongside top laner Zest and botlane Duduhh-Ackerman. Both squads tied at 4-3 series records in the league stage—LOS 4th with even 9-9 games, Fluxo W7M 5th at 9-7—though Fluxo flashed upset potential with victories over FURIA and LOUD via mid laner cody and supports Momochi-ProDelta. eventMetadata.context_description: Ilia Topuria leads trader consensus at 62% implied probability as the reigning UFC lightweight champion, having claimed the vacant belt with a dominant first-round knockout of former titleholder Charles Oliveira at UFC 317 last summer before vacating his featherweight strap. His undefeated 17-0 record and explosive finishing power fuel expectations he'll unify against interim champion Justin Gaethje (4.7%) in the blockbuster UFC White House main event on June 14, with recent promotional renders and fighter faceoffs heightening anticipation. Charles Oliveira (11.1%) and Arman Tsarukyan (10.5%) trail as top challengers, bolstered by Oliveira's resilience despite the title loss and Tsarukyan's submission win over Dan Hooker late last year, positioning them for potential shots post-unification amid a crowded contender field including Benoît Saint Denis (7.2%). No defenses have occurred in 2026 yet, but Topuria's planned activity and the division's momentum sustain his frontrunner status through year-end.

markets.question: Exact Score: Stade Lavallois Mayenne FC 2 - 1 US Boulogne Côte d'Opale?
markets.description: In the upcoming Ligue 2 game between Stade Lavallois Mayenne FC and US Boulogne Côte d'Opale, scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Stade Lavallois Mayenne FC vs. US Boulogne Côte d'Opale match originally scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-05 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-05 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Republican Party at 83.5% implied probability to win Wisconsin's 5th Congressional District House seat, driven by the district's strong R+11 Cook Partisan Voter Index and incumbent Rep. Scott Fitzgerald's consistent landslide victories—64% in 2024 against Ben Steinhoff, 64% in 2022, and 60% in 2020. Fitzgerald faces no Republican primary challengers ahead of the June 1 filing deadline and August 11 primary, while Democrats field Steinhoff, a repeat loser, and newcomer Andrew Beck in their primary. Nonpartisan ratings from Cook Political (Solid Republican), Inside Elections, and Sabato's Crystal Ball reinforce the race's safety for the GOP, with Milwaukee suburbs' slight leftward trend insufficient to threaten. Late developments like a high-profile Democratic recruit or national midterm dynamics could narrow odds before the November 3 general election.
